mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that is designed for ease of deployment and management using Juju. It is ideal for users looking for a robust Kubernetes solution that integrates seamlessly with Ubuntu and offers modern metrics and monitoring capabilities. Key trade-offs include the requirement for Juju and Ubuntu, which may not be familiar to all users.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is required to deploy Charmed Kubernetes. This step installs the Juju client on your local machine.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of Juju.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Juju controller
steps.description:
This step initializes the Juju controller, which manages the deployment of Charmed Kubernetes.

```bash

juju bootstrap localhost

```

> Bootstraps a local Juju controller.

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle, which includes all necessary components.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes

```

> Deploys the latest version of Charmed Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
Check the status of the deployed services to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Displays the status of all deployed services.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure additional features such as monitoring and metrics.

```bash

juju add-relation kubernetes-master:metrics grafana:metrics

```

> Links the Kubernetes master to Grafana for monitoring.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Charmed Kubernetes deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss.

```bash

juju destroy-controller localhost --destroy-all-models

```

> Destroys the Juju controller and all models.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

juju remove-application kubernetes

```

> Removes the Kubernetes application from Juju.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade the Charmed Kubernetes deployment to the latest version.

```bash

juju upgrade-charm kubernetes-master

juju upgrade-charm kubernetes-worker

juju status

```

> Upgrades the Kubernetes master charm.
> Upgrades the Kubernetes worker charm.
> Verify that all services are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju bootstrap failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Juju controller cannot connect to the local environment.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your local environment is properly configured and that you have sufficient resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubernetes-master is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes master component has not fully initialized.

**Fix:**
Check the Juju logs for the kubernetes-master application using 'juju debug-log'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'relation not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the required relations between services are not established.

**Fix:**
Ensure that all necessary relations are added using 'juju add-relation'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'resource not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if a required resource was deleted or not created.

**Fix:**
Check the status of your applications and ensure all resources are correctly deployed.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31 features including improved metrics collection and enhanced RBAC capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy metrics endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure that all metrics configurations are updated to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'juju status' indicating that all services are active and running without errors.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a compatible environment with sufficient resources.
